evals.prompt: Use engineer-literature to read this English abstract for my Chinese engineering paper. Tell me what it contributes and how I can cite it in the introduction.
evals.expected_output: The assistant explains in Chinese what problem the paper solves, the core method, evidence strength, limitations, and where it can be used in a Chinese introduction. It separates facts from inference if only an abstract is available.

assertions.name: chinese_research_card
assertions.description: The output is a Chinese paper card focused on engineering research value, not only a generic summary.

assertions.name: writing_bridge
assertions.description: The response includes how to use the paper in introduction or related-work writing.

assertions.name: source_scope_caveat
assertions.description: The response notes limits when only an abstract or partial source is available.

evals.prompt: Find the latest frontier papers on physics-informed graph neural networks for engineering dynamics and give me Chinese related-work material.
evals.expected_output: The assistant searches or verifies current sources before naming latest papers, groups papers by technical route, and produces Chinese related-work material with verified publication/preprint status and caveats.

assertions.name: verifies_latest_sources
assertions.description: The assistant does not rely only on memory for latest/frontier literature.

assertions.name: method_family_grouping
assertions.description: The output groups papers by technical routes and evidence level rather than dumping a chronological list.

assertions.name: chinese_related_work
assertions.description: The response converts the scan into Chinese related-work or manuscript positioning material.
